<!-- generated by session_final_report for scaffold/20260709_081340 at 2026-07-09 23:26 UTC -->

# ScaFFold I/O Annotation + Optimization — Final Report

**App:** LBANN ScaFFold (PyTorch 3D U-Net on procedurally generated fractal volumes)
**System:** Tuolumne (AMD MI300A APU, Cray PE, Flux)
**Shape:** 8 nodes x 4 GPUs = 32 ranks, `problem_scale=6`
**Tracer:** dftracer @ develop, dftracer-utils @ develop, built into the app's own venv

---

## 1. Headline result

The best configuration is **`dataloader_num_workers=4` + `async_save=1` + `hardlink_best=1`**,
plus a source fix that stops re-reading the best checkpoint every epoch.

| metric | baseline | best | delta |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| wall time (960 epochs, paired control) | 953.9 s | **930.2 s** | -2.5% (checkpoint fixes alone) |
| wall time vs pre-optimization opt1 | 1052.9 s | 930.2 s | **-11.7%** |
| training time (1200 epochs) | 749.9 s | 497.5 s | **-33.7%** (dataloader fix) |

The dataloader fix is the dominant win. Its measured size depends on filesystem contention:
**-15.3% to -33.7%**, because the unoptimized dataloader is synchronous and therefore tracks
contention, while the optimized one does not (per-epoch spread 26.8% vs 0.6%).

---

## 2. Two measurement traps that invalidated earlier conclusions

These are the most transferable findings of the session.

### 2.1 The app's own metric excluded the thing being optimized
`total_train_time` (and the FOM derived from it) **excludes checkpointing**. At 960 epochs opt1
reports 400.5 s of "training" against **1052.9 s wall** — the missing ~652 s *is* the checkpoint
stall. Every percentage computed on the app metric was blind to the target. **Measure wall clock.**

### 2.2 A control from an hour ago measures cluster contention, not your change
Two runs of the **identical** control config, ~30 min apart, measured `train=140.96 s` and
`train=293.46 s` — a **2x swing**. Consequences:

* `hardlink_best` first appeared as a **+26% regression**; re-measured against a *concurrent*
  control it is a **-2.5% win**.
* Full-die core affinity first appeared as a **+22.5% regression**; split into halves and
  measured against concurrent controls, both are **neutral** (+0.4% and +1.8%).

**Rule adopted:** launch the variant and its control simultaneously, on separate same-size
allocations, with identical epoch counts. Quote the paired delta. Never compare across hours.

---

## 3. Measured optimization results

All at 32 ranks, `problem_scale=6`, fixed epoch count per comparison.

| lever | level | measurement | verdict |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `dataloader_num_workers` 0 -> 4 | L1 | train 749.9 -> 497.5 s (1200 ep) | **KEEP** -33.7%; POSIX time -86% (5666 -> 801 s); identical checkpoint count => real overlap, not less work |
| best-loss cache (stop `torch.load` of `checkpoint_best.pth` every epoch) | L1 | wall -1.9% | **KEEP** |
| `async_save=1` (CheckFreq snapshot/persist split) | L1 | wall -4.4% cumulative | **KEEP**, but saturates: the 496 ms write exceeds the 415 ms epoch, so it pipelines only one epoch deep |
| `hardlink_best=1` (os.link instead of 256 MB copyfile) | L1 | wall 953.9 -> 930.2 s, paired | **KEEP** -2.5%; removes 25.5 GB of duplicate writes |
| `checkpoint_interval` 1 -> 4 | L1 | train -23.1% | **REJECT** — "do-less": writes 75% fewer checkpoints (78192 -> 19692 events) |
| stacked (workers + checkpoint_interval) | L1 | 503.2 vs 497.5 s | **REJECT** — does not compound; within noise of the dataloader fix alone |
| `async_save=1` at `problem_scale=5` | L1 | +36% | **REJECT** at that scale — nothing to overlap (checkpoints were 0.05% of I/O) |
| `ckpt_stage_dir=/dev/shm` + `shutil.move` | L1/L3 | wall +6.3%, paired | **REJECT** — tmpfs and Lustre are different devices, so the move degrades to read+write: the 256 MB is written **twice** |
| full-die core affinity (`-p cores_per_node=96 gpus_per_node=4`) | L3 | +0.4% train, paired | **NO EFFECT** — `pin_memory=True` already set; launcher binding already adequate |
| `OMP_NUM_THREADS=6 OMP_PROC_BIND=close` | L3 | +1.8% train, paired | **NO EFFECT** |
| Lustre striping / Data-on-MDT | L3 | not run | **NO-OP** — `lfs getstripe -d` shows a PFL with a 64 KB DoM first component already provisioned |

---

## 4. Where the time actually goes (best config, per rank per epoch)

Epoch = 415 ms.

| op | per-rank per-epoch | note |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `checkpointing.save_checkpoint` | **548 ms** | all 32 ranks |
| `_write_to_disk` | 496 ms | **rank 0 only** — the other 31 block on it |
| `_run_training_batch` | 354 ms | actual compute |
| `data_loading.__getitem__` | 47 ms | already fixed by the dataloader change |
| MPI (all) | 0.4 s total across the whole run | **not** a bottleneck |

**The remaining ceiling is checkpoint bytes.** The checkpoint is 256.4 MB, written every epoch:
**240 GB per 960-epoch run from a single rank** at ~517 MB/s. `async_save` cannot hide a write
that is longer than an epoch.

`MPI_Barrier` looked like the #1 bottleneck (99.3% of MPI time) in an early 2-second run. At a
realistic run length it is **16.8 s across 32 ranks against 749.9 s of training (~2%)**. Ranking
bottlenecks from a run whose training phase is a few seconds is actively misleading.

---

## 5. Not done / next steps

Ordered by expected value against the 240 GB ceiling:

1. **Shrink the checkpoint.** 256 MB is model params `P` + Adam's two moments `2P`. Casting the
   moments to bf16 takes `3P -> 2P` (~-33% bytes) with no change in training work.
   *Eisenman et al., Check-N-Run, USENIX NSDI 2022.*
2. **Decouple the PFS drain.** Multi-level checkpointing only pays when the local write and the
   drain to Lustre run on **separate** threads with pipeline depth > 1. The naive synchronous
   staging tried here was strictly worse. *Moody et al., SCR, SC 2010.*
3. **`torch_amp=1`** (bf16 compute). `_run_training_batch` is 71% of the epoch. A prior attempt was
   truncated by allocation expiry. Note `batch_size` is **per rank**: `batch_size * world_size`
   must not exceed the training-sample count (`4 x 32 = 128 > 112` raises `ValueError`).
4. **Shard the dataset.** 11.4 M POSIX metadata calls and ~496 `fopen`/rank/epoch come from
   per-sample `.npy` opens. Now clearly second-order behind checkpoint bytes.
   *Devarajan et al., DLIO, CCGrid 2021.*

Every one of these needs a **concurrent control**: at a 2x contention swing, nothing smaller is
trustworthy.

---

## 6. Environment (the precondition for all of the above)

The single largest source of wasted effort was an environment that diverged from what the app
itself declares. ScaFFold's own `scripts/install-tuolumne.sh` and `scripts/scaffold-tuolumne.job`
specify **python/3.11.5** (not the site default 3.13.2), `.[rocmwci]` wheels, a `patchelf` of
torch's MPI SONAME, and a specific `LD_PRELOAD`.

Symptoms of ignoring them, all of which were misdiagnosed as something else first:

| symptom | actual cause |
| --- | --- |
| run exits 0 with an **empty trace** | wrong python => GLIBCXX/RPATH mismatch => `import dftracer.dftracer` raised an ImportError that `logger.py` swallows, leaving a `NoOpProfiler` |
| `torch.cuda.init()` dies: `Error in dlopen: libcaffe2_nvrtc.so` | missing `libomp` preload + `torch/lib` absent from `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` (gotcha intercepts `dlopen`, defeating torch's `$ORIGIN` RPATH). **Not** "ROCProfiler is broken" |
| `double free or corruption (!prev)` at exit | dftracer linked `libmpi_cray` while the app preloads `libmpi_gnu` — two MPI runtimes |

Fixes, now codified in `install_stack.sh`: one script installs app + dftracer + dftracer-utils into
one venv in one module env; `CC`/`CXX` bound to the **GNU** MPICH wrappers the app's wheels use;
MPI passed explicitly (no HDF5 — ScaFFold does not use it); `LDFLAGS="-ldl"`; dftracer installed
**before** dftracer-utils; `PIP_INDEX_URL` forced to the WCI index.

**Known benign:** an exit-time `SIGABRT` (134). The app requires preloading both ROCm's LLVM
`libomp` (torch's `libmagma`) and MKL (`cblas_gemm_f16f16f32`); those are two OpenMP runtimes and,
with dftracer's gotcha loaded, abort at exit **after** the trace is flushed. Runner scripts
tolerate rc=134 only and validate the trace instead.

---

## 7. Annotation

11 files / 45 functions initially; validation raised real defects (4 files mischaracterized as
"off the hot path", metadata emitted as literal strings, a missing `finalize()` on the
`generate_fractals` path). Final coverage: **100/166 functions (60.2%)**, hot path complete.

`initialize_log()` must be called exactly **once per process**: `cli.py` imports both `worker` and
`generate_fractals`, so initializing in both double-frees the singleton on `finalize()`.

Baseline trace: **925,828 events, 64 processes**, training annotations on all 32 ranks, with MPI
events visible for the first time once dftracer was linked against the app's MPI.

---

## 8. Contents of this folder

| path | what |
| --- | --- |
| `REPORT.md` | this document — what was done and what was measured |
| `README.md` | how to reproduce by hand, and the traps to avoid |
| `CONVERSATION.md` | narrative: what was tried, what was wrong, what corrected it |
| `results/results.csv` | every measured run; **read `results/README.md` first** |
| `params/` | the exact config for every case in `results.csv` |
| `scripts/` | `install_stack.sh` (whole stack, one venv), `env.sh`, `run_trial2.sh`, dispatcher |
| `patches/` | annotation diff + the checkpoint optimization edits (see `patches/README.md`) |
| `plan/` | the executed pipeline plan and its dated changelog |
| `logs/` | build/run logs referenced by the cases |
| `PERFORMANCE.md` | per-step cost/retry profile of the pipeline itself |

**If you read one thing:** `results/README.md`. Only `PAIRED` rows support a delta.
